key_insight: A non-overlapping domain decomposition method turns the coupled Stokes-Biot fluid-poroelastic problem into a parallelizable interface problem solved efficiently with GMRES.
engineering_relevance: This gives a scalable way to simulate fluid–poroelastic interaction in engineering systems where free-flow and porous media meet, such as biomechanics and filtration.
method: Mixed finite-element discretization for Stokes, elasticity, and Darcy subproblems
Lagrange multipliers enforce interface continuity and coupling conditions
Schur complement reduces the global system to an interface solve with local parallel subdomain solves
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments and a fields-of-value convergence analysis to demonstrate the solver’s performance and positive-definite interface operator.
watch_item: Performance depends on stable mixed discretizations and careful implementation of the interface multipliers and subdomain solves.
paper_title: Domain decomposition methods for the Stokes-Biot model of fluid-poroelastic structure interaction
